revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:C9HxkpSVYAAQdKL.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|42|N|135|49|14|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=倭恩智神社}} |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = 春日造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんぢじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。[[新撰姓氏録]]に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭淹知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、これとの関係が想定されるが、そちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === 創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする
[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月正六位上『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』
[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）正月叙位
[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位
[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:C9HxowFUQAAJET3.jpg|標石 ファイル:C9Hxr59U0AAU6T2.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:C9HxwM-UAAA-eUU.jpg|本殿 == 外部リンク == [http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031206-01.html 倭恩智神社] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}}

{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:C9HxkpSVYAAQdKL.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|42|N|135|49|14|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=倭恩智神社}} |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = 春日造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんぢじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で[[旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。[[新撰姓氏録]]に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭淹知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、これとの関係が想定されるが、そちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === 創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする
[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月正六位上『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』
[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）正月叙位
[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位
[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:C9HxowFUQAAJET3.jpg|標石 ファイル:C9Hxr59U0AAU6T2.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:C9HxwM-UAAA-eUU.jpg|本殿 == 外部リンク == [http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031206-01.html 倭恩智神社] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}}

{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:C9HxkpSVYAAQdKL.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）

[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月正六位上『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』
[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）正月叙位
[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位
[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:C9HxowFUQAAJET3.jpg|標石 ファイル:C9Hxr59U0AAU6T2.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:C9HxwM-UAAA-eUU.jpg|本殿 == 外部リンク == [http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031206-01.html 倭恩智神社] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}}

{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする *[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）1月正六位上（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） *[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）1月叙位 *[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位 *[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Yamato Onchi Shrine'''|倭恩智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Yamato Onchi Shrine'''|倭恩智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Yamato Onchi Shrine'''|倭恩智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Yamato Onchi Shrine'''|倭恩智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Yamato Onchi Shrine'''|倭恩智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする *[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）1月正六位上（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） *[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）1月叙位 *[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位 *[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

revision.text:

page|ja|倭恩智神社|version=106919248|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする *[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）1月正六位上（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） *[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）1月叙位 *[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位 *[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月9日]] |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は[[崇神天皇]]7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「奄知造」の名があり、「[[大和国]]神別奄知造　[[天津彦根命]]十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]（旧[[河内国]]）に[[恩智神社]]（式内[[名神大社]]）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは[[中臣氏]]との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を[[崇神天皇]]7年とする *[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）1月正六位上（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） *[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）1月叙位 *[[永徳]]元年（[[1381年]]）2月叙位 *[[昭和19年]]（[[1944年]]）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 == 歴史 == === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 == 境内 == ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 歴史 === === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 === 境内 === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 歴史 === === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 === 境内 === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やまとおんちじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005003156 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県天理市海知町32', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:倭恩智神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 歴史 === === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 === 境内 === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やまとおんちじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005003156 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県天理市海知町32', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:倭恩智神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 歴史 === === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 === 境内 === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やまとおんちじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005003156 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県天理市海知町32', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:倭恩智神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 倭恩智神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}海知町32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = 建凝命 |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月9日|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''倭恩智神社'''（やまとおんちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 歴史 === === 創祀 === 創祀は{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とされ、『磯城郡誌』に倭俺知氏の祖を祀れるものとある。『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「奄知造」の名があり、「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}神別奄知造　{{jalink|天津彦根命}}十四世孫建凝命之後也」と記されている。その倭俺知氏が祖神・建凝命を祀ったのが当社であると推測される。また当社と同じ恩智を名乗る神社に、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}（旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}）に{{jalink|恩智神社|Q11492258}}（式内{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）があり、関係が想定されるが、こちらは{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}との関わりが深い神社であり、祭神も当社とは異なる。 === 略歴 === *創始を{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年とする *{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）1月正六位上（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） *{{jalink|天慶|Q1327340}}3年（{{jalink|940年|Q23830}}）1月叙位 *{{jalink|永徳|Q1145805}}元年（{{jalink|1381年|Q6331}}）2月叙位 *{{jalink|昭和19年}}（{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}）大和海軍航空隊飛行場の建設計画により集落南の現在地に移転 === 境内 === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|社叢 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 9|Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}} (an {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} (former {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} *{{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') *{{jalink|Tengyō|Q1327340}} 3 ({{jalink|940|Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1381|Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{jalink|Shōwa 19|Q5268|1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill||lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとおんちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]
