Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

link|Q43594603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamato Onchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamatoonji Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Kaichi-chō 32 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 42.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 15 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Takenori-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 9|Q2856|WD=Q2856|1=September 9}} |神事 = }} '''Yamato Onchi Shrine''' (やまとおんちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === === Foundation === Its foundation is said to date to year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the ''Shiki-gunshi'' states that it enshrines the ancestor of the Yamato Onchi clan. The ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the name "Onchi-no-Miyatsuko" and states: "Yamato no Kambetsu Onchi-no-Miyatsuko are descendants in the 14th generation of Takenori-no-Mikoto, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}." It is inferred that this shrine was where the Yamato Onchi clan enshrined their ancestral deity Takenori-no-Mikoto. There is also {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}} (an {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} listed in the Engishiki) in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}), bearing the same "Onchi" name, and a relationship has been suggested; however, that shrine has strong ties with the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}, and its enshrined deity differs from that of this shrine. === Chronology === *Foundation is given as year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} *{{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), 1st month: promoted to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') *{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}), 1st month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1381|Q6331|WD=Q6331|1=1381}}), 2nd month: rank conferred *{{ill|lt=Shōwa 19|Q5268|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}}: relocated to its current site south of the settlement due to plans for construction of the Yamato Naval Air Corps airfield === Precincts === ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary ファイル:Yamatoonji Jinja Shaso.jpg|Sacred grove === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamato Onchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
